titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to 19th Century French Bookstore Poster Collection
titlestmt.author: Camila Zorrilla Tessler

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 3, 2025 at 23:00

did.unittitle: 19th Century French Bookstore Poster collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Librairie Benoit Forgeot, using funds from the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2023.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The 19th Century French Bookstore Poster collection documents a variety of posters (or affiches) used by bookstores in Paris to advertise books, pamphlets, and other merchandise.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: 19th Century French Bookstore Poster Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 19th Century French Bookstore Poster collection consists primarily of posters and lithographs produced between 1829 and 1896. There is one woodblock used for printing posters. The posters were largely produced by the publisher or editor, and editors are noted at the item level scope and content note. Posters do not contain information on which bookstore may have displayed them.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creators of the posters are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on the use of these materials. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Curator permission needed to access these materials due to fragility.
